self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can keep the canonical truth anchor and workbench detail useful by resolving the remaining narrative historical Runtime follow-ups directly instead of crashing or surfacing invalid state.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP bounded result adds a read-only narrative legacy Runtime follow-up compatibility path to the shared state resolver and workbench follow-up detail fallback.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP case, the remaining narrative `*-runtime-followup.md` artifacts still crashed the canonical report and surfaced as invalid workbench follow-up state.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Future report, composition, and workbench work can inspect the remaining narrative historical Runtime follow-up family directly without weakening current Runtime v0 boundaries.

decision.rationale: The shared truth anchor should resolve the remaining narrative historical Runtime follow-ups as read-only artifacts instead of crashing or surfacing invalid state.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
